---
title: "New 10.04 User: Wireless connected but can't connect to Internet."
date: 2010-08-18
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless
---

### Post by bombozz on 2010-08-18
Hi...I am new ubuntu user. NM reports that my wireless connection has been established, but I am not able to connect to internet. I am not even able to ping any of my DNS servers...not even to my gateway (192.168.1.1). 

My wired connection works fine. 

Any help would be appreciated. 

Details:
--------

My cat /etc/resolv.conf results:
# Generated by NetworkManager
nameserver 68.87.72.134
nameserver 68.87.77.134
nameserver 8.8.8.8


My route -n results:
Kernel IP routing table
Destination     Gateway         Genmask         Flags Metric Ref    Use Iface
192.168.1.0     0.0.0.0         255.255.255.0   U     2      0        0 wlan0
169.254.0.0     0.0.0.0         255.255.0.0     U     1000   0        0 wlan0
0.0.0.0         192.168.1.1     0.0.0.0         UG    100    0        0 wlan0

Thanks in advance!

---

### Post by Iowan on 2010-08-18
Results of **ifconfig -a** ?

---

### Post by wanwarlock on 2010-08-19
hi,

i had the same issue as you. managed to update fine but can't use internet. 

in firefox:

type about:config in the address bar

find network.dns.disableIPv6 

and set it to TRUE


save and restart ff


that should sort out the internet issue, it did for me

---
